exemplar.title: To what extent have the interest rate hikes by the FED and the fluctuations in value of the USD impacted the US balance of payments between 2022 and early 2024 ?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent have the interest rate hikes by the FED and the fluctuations in value of the USD impacted the US balance of payments between 2022 and early 2024 ?

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Title clearly identifies independent and dependent variables with precise time frame, enhancing focus.
Research question is explicitly stated, time-bounded and linked to analysis.
weaknesses: Dual âto what extentâ phrasing risks breadth and scope remains somewhat broad.
Methodology description omits explicit criteria for data selection and lacks primary data.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigationâs purpose and focus are communicated accurately and effectively, and the research question is clear and connected to the discussion, placing it at the upper end of the 3â4 band. However, the scope is still broad without explicit delimitation, and the methodological section lacks rigor in data selection, preventing a higher mark. A score of 4/6 best reflects these strengths and limitations.

strengths: Clear theoretical framework with coherent definitions of BoP components.
Effective use of BEA and FRED data to support macroeconomic context.
Terminology (e.g., âcurrent account deficitâ, âportfolio investmentâ) is accurate and consistent.
weaknesses: Reliance on non-academic sources (e.g., Wikipedia, bank websites) weakens depth.
Occasional inaccuracies (CHF/EUR quotation errors) and limited discussion of capital vs financial account.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates clear knowledge of topic and coherent use of terminology, but many source applications remain descriptive and some sources lack academic depth. Overall, this aligns with the lower end of the 3â4 band, warranting 3/6.

strengths: Majority of research draws on credible macroeconomic databases directly relevant to the question.
Critical appraisal of source bias and acknowledgment of correlation vs. causation enhance rigour.
Line of argument on the J-curve effect provides a reasoned structure.
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely narrative without statistical testing or magnitude estimates.
Evaluation of alternative explanations (e.g., fiscal policy, energy prices) is superficial.
Conclusions are only partially supported by the analysis presented.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research is generally appropriate and relevant, with clear analysis and a reasoned argument, but depth and critical evaluation are limited. These characteristics fit the lower part of the 4â6 band, so a score of 4/12 is appropriate.

strengths: Report follows EE conventions (title page, contents, numbered sections, bibliography).
Detailed table of contents aids navigation and demonstrates structure.
weaknesses: Inconsistent heading styles and pagination errors disrupt flow.
Broken URLs and erratic footnote numbering affect formatting consistency.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation is clearly structured and largely follows required conventions, but minor layout and formatting inconsistencies prevent a flawless execution. This aligns with the criteria for a band-3 performance, meriting 3/4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly stated and time-bounded, showing a clear link to the ensuing analysis.  Nevertheless, the phrasing "to what extent" across two separate causal variables risks breadth, and the essay later struggles to keep the discussion tightly centred on causal estimation versus descriptive trends.  Thus the question is clear but only partially focused, meriting the upper end of the 3-4 band rather than the highest band.

The title, introduction and table of contents clearly signal that the investigation will examine the effects of FED rate hikes and USD movements on the U S balance of payments over a precise 2022-24 period.  The focus on two identifiable independent variables and one dependent variable makes the purpose understandable.  However, the scope remains quite broad (two policy channels, several BoP sub-accounts) and no explicit delimitation of which parts of the BoP will receive priority is offered, so complete precision is lacking.  Overall communication and appropriateness are adequate rather than exemplary, fitting the middle band.

Most cited graphs and macroeconomic series (imports, exports, current account, CPI, exchange rate) are pertinent to the investigation and are applied in the text.  However, many uses are descriptiveâdata are quoted to illustrate a statement rather than interrogated.  Some sources (Bankrate, Wikipedia) lack academic depth.  Application is therefore mostly but not consistently relevant, supporting a score of 3 in the 3-4 band.

The report follows EE conventions: title page, abstract substitute, table of contents, numbered sections, bibliography.  Some pagination errors and inconsistent heading styles are visible, and figure placement occasionally disrupts flow.  These minor issues stop it from reaching flawless execution, so a 3 is appropriate.

Word count, title and section breaks are provided; most citations follow a consistent format and the bibliography is alphabetised.  Nonetheless, URLs are often broken across lines, footnote numbering is erratic, and several in-text figure captions lack numbering.  Layout considerations are largely but not perfectly respected, placing the work at 3 in the higher band.

A line of argument is presentâmonetary tightening initially worsened, then improved the current account via the J-curveâyet evaluation is thin.  Alternative explanations (fiscal policy, global energy prices) are not critically weighed, and the concluding judgement largely restates earlier description.  The work therefore provides a reasoned but only partially critical discussion, meriting 4.

The essay links rate hikes to exchange-rate paths and trade data, and attempts to interpret short-run vs long-run effects via the J-curve.  However, analysis remains largely narrative: no statistical tests, elasticities or magnitude estimates are provided, and confounding variables are acknowledged but not investigated.  Conclusions are only partially supported, so depth meets but does not exceed the basic expectation of the 4-6 band.

The student demonstrates sound understanding of the balance-of-payments structure, J-curve, Marshall-Lerner condition and the monetary-policy transmission mechanism.  Nevertheless, occasional inaccuracies (confusing CHF/EUR quotation, typographical errors, limited discussion of capital vs financial account) show that knowledge is clear but not fully coherent, matching the descriptor for 3.

The majority of data employed come from credible macroeconomic databases (BEA, FRED, ECB, Trading Economics) and relate directly to interest rates, exchange rates and BoP outcomes, showing appropriateness.  Yet some journalistic or generic web sources dilute rigour, and no primary material is gathered.  Relevance is clear but not comprehensive, aligning with the lower edge of the 4-6 band.

The student lists secondary data sources, provides a brief bias appraisal and identifies graphs and macro-series as analytical tools.  Yet there is no explicit data-selection criterion, no explanation of why particular quarterly series were preferred, and no primary data.  Method description of how relationships will be tested stays qualitative.  Hence methodology is generally relevant but only partially appropriate, placing it mid-band with a score of 3.

Economic terminology such as "current account deficit", "asset price channel", "price elasticity of demand" and "portfolio investment" is used consistently and largely accurately.  Minor spelling errors do not impede meaning.  Overall the use of terminology satisfies the upper part of the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: Table of contents is detailed and follows expected structure, aiding navigation.
Transmission mechanism figure correctly sourced but lacks a figure number for reference.
Title clearly identifies the variables (FED rate hikes, USD fluctuations) and dependent variable (US BoP) with time frame, enhancing focus.
Research question is explicit and timeâbounded, but the dual âto what extentâ framing risks breadth; consider narrowing focus to one causal channel.
Word count is correctly stated and under 4000, meeting IB formatting requirements.
Spacing in the TOC table is inconsistent; consider equalizing column widths and row heights to improve readability.
Footer shows consistent page numbering, satisfying formatting conventions.
Introduction provides strong macroeconomic context with GDP and CPI data. This grounding clarifies relevance.
Reliance on nonâacademic sources (e.g., U.S. Bank) weakens depth; integrate peerâreviewed journal articles for stronger support.
Final sentence of introduction is incomplete; finish the thought on rate hikes to maintain narrative flow.
Graph of FED rate hikes is wellâchosen, visually illustrating policy frequency.
Figure lacks axis labels and a numbered caption; add clear labels and reference as âFigure 1â in text.
Research rationale paragraph reads narratively; streamline to explicitly link choice of question to gaps in literature.
Methodology lists secondary sources but omits criteria for data selection (e.g., period, frequency); define these to improve rigor.
Critical appraisal of The Economist and FT bias shows reflective awareness of source reliability.
Citation of Wikipedia in methodology undermines academic credibility; replace with peerâreviewed sources.
Discussion of Keynesian model is descriptive; explicitly connect its assumptions to expected BoP responses under rate hikes.
Typo and truncation after ânatural stages in theâ; proofread to ensure completeness.
Acknowledging correlation vs causation in tools section demonstrates strong critical thinking.
Footnote numbering and formatting vary across pages; standardize to one style (e.g., superscript) for consistency.
Define balance of payments identity formally (e.g., $$	ext{CA}+	ext{KA}+	ext{FA}=0$$) to strengthen methodological clarity.
Theoretical framework section concisely defines BoP components, showing clear theoretical understanding.
Link monetary transmission channels explicitly to BoP subâaccounts in the framework to prep for analysis.
Use of BEA and FRED data on current account enhances credibility of evidence base.
Graph on page 9 lacks figure number and caption; include âFigure 2â and descriptive title.
Consider discussing global energy price shocks as a confounding factor in the 2022 current account deficit spike.
Distinction between floating and fixed regimes is overviewed but application to USD could be deepened.
Linking external debt concerns to BoP illustrates strong understanding of macro dependencies.
Integrate a simple regression or correlation coefficient analysis to support narrative on FX and trade linkages.
Application of J-curve diagram on page 12 effectively ties theory to US outcomes.
Explanation of J-curve effect ends abruptly; complete sentence to clarify second phase of adjustment.
How might the Marshall-Lerner condition ($$Î·_X+Î·_M>1$$) empirically hold in this period? Provide elasticity estimates.
Historical comparison of 2015â18 rate hikes provides valuable perspective on policy intensity.
Inline math markup for â11thâ is inconsistent; replace with $$11^{\text{th}}$$ or plain text to maintain clarity.
Discuss lag structure in transmission mechanism: specify expected delays between rate hikes and BoP response.
Spelling error âmonterayâ in text below figure; proofread to correct to âmonetary.â
Link to global economy impact is insightful, showing awareness of USDâs role beyond domestic BoP.
